description:
# Why

bl-ffb4 introduced master_url mode, which materializes a balls-owned
git clone of the task hub at `.balls/state-repo/` (`state_repo::
STATE_REPO_REL`). Like the `.balls/code-refs` cache before bl-c4e2,
`.balls/state-repo` is created at the repo root but is absent from
both lines of defense:

- `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`: not in any `wanted` list, so a
  non-bare master_url repo carries an untracked `.balls/state-repo/`
  tree. A subsequent `git add -A` silently commits an entire clone of
  the task hub into the project.
- `review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS`: not listed, so the `bl review`
  squash backstop (`commit_touches_runtime`) would not reject a
  state-repo tree that slipped past a stale `.gitignore`.

This is the exact gap bl-c4e2 closed for `.balls/code-refs` and
bl-0b33 finished for stealth mode â€” `.balls/state-repo` is the third
runtime path under `.balls/` with the same shape and was simply
missed because it predates that review pass.

# Scope

- `review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS`: add `.balls/state-repo` (the
  backstop is mode-independent â€” a path that never exists just never
  matches, so no gating needed here).
- `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`: add `.balls/state-repo` to the
  `wanted` list. Check whether it should be unconditional (like
  `.balls/local` / `.balls/code-refs`) or gated: it is materialized by
  master_url resolution, which is orthogonal to stealth mode, so
  unconditional is the likely answer â€” verify against `state_repo`'s
  setup path before placing it.
- A unit test in `review_safety_tests.rs` confirming a commit touching
  `.balls/state-repo/...` is flagged by `commit_touches_runtime`,
  mirroring `commit_touches_runtime_flags_code_refs_cache`.

# Out of scope

The structural fix â€” a single source of truth so a new `.balls/`
sidecar can't be half-wired again â€” is tracked separately. If that
ball lands first, this one collapses to a one-line table entry; do
whichever is ready first, they are not ordered.

# Origin

Follow-up from the bl-c4e2 / bl-0b33 review passes. Flagged as the
known parallel gap in bl-c4e2's commit body ("Out of scope:
.balls/state-repo has the same shape and the same gap").
title: Gitignore + runtime-paths cover .balls/state-repo
